title: Excellent condition Subaru Forester 5-speed manual trans.
description: This SUV needs nothing, it's completely turn-key. I just brought it to the mechanic* to check the fluids (brake, clutch, transmission, etc) and the brake pads. The mechanic said the brake pads are at 40% and the fluids are all fine, there was nothing for him to do. He recommended pads should be replaced this summer along with the oil change. Beyond that, the car needs absolutely nothing. It has new high and low-beam LEDs that increase visibility without blinding oncoming vehicles. Also recently equipped with top of the line fog lights by Diode Dynamics (SS3 pods along with vehicle-specific Diode Dynamics wiring harness) for enhanced visibility when driving in the fog and snow. Fog lights are amber in color and offer two brightness settings. They are wired to an in-cabin switch near the interior light dimmer. I've also replaced the turn signals with amber LEDs as well as the brake lights. Replaced: Axle shaft and CV joint, lower control arms, steering rack, sway bar, outer tie rod, front axle in May 2025. New cabin filter, steering wheel cover, clutch/gas/brake pedals, seat covers. Brand new tires, installed at Walmart, comes with lifetime tire balancing. 2,400lb capacity tow hitch. I’ve pulled a U-Haul trailer with zero issues. Bluetooth, CD player, AM/FM/Sat radio. Controls for calls, music controls, and cruise control on steering wheel. Regular full synthetic oil changes, proactively maintained, all parts replaced/repaired at regular intervals. Has full spare tire and jack, never been used. Has aux input and USB jack (in arm rest). Has additional 12v/120w cigarette lighter/auxiliary outlet in rear cargo area. No rust, 2nd owner, regularly washed including undercarriage. Terrific in all the snow we’ve had this winter. Will be great in the spring showers too. My mom is giving me her 2018 Forester, the last year they made it with manual transmission, so I need to pass this on to someone else who will appreciate and take good care of it. Terrific vehicle. This model is PZEV meaning that it's designed to meet California's strict air quality standards by producing exceptionally low exhaust emissions and zero fuel evaporative emissions. Blemishes: superficial scratches on rear bumper, headlights slightly hazy due to age but can be buffed out ($40), the new LED headlights make that just a cosmetic issue as nighttime visibility could not be better. Original owner replaced cargo area floor with wood, I covered in cowhide so it would look better and make a nicer surface for transporting (see pix). Never had an issue with it. Car has been used as an SUV is intended, it’s made many trips to Lowes and Home Depot and hauled some wood and cinder blocks. As a result there are some scratches on the back of the arm rest, some marks on the interior fabric (under the seat covers). Size is perfect for navigating the city or country, it can squeeze into small parking spots but you can also throw your road bike in the back with some camping gear (or an inflatable twin mattress) and hit the road. If you continue to stay on top of regular oil changes and basic maintenance this car will easily take you over 300k miles. *happy to provide mechanic contact info to confirm service record

title: 2005 Pontiac gto aps twin turbo 6spd, many upgrades 10 sec car!
description: ******READ DESCRIPTION THOROUGHLY BEFORE CALLING ME!*******DO NOT MAKE ME ANY OFFERS UNLESS IN PERSON WITH THE CASH IN HAND READY TO BUY, IF YOU SEND ME A STUPID OFFER VIA MSG I WILL BLOCK YOU. I AM ONLY INTERESTED IN SPEAKING TO SERIOUS BUYERS. DON'T TXT ME PICK UP THE PHONE IF INTERESTED AND CALL, I DON'T ANSWER TXT'S PERIOD. I AM A LICENSED PA USED CAR DEALER THIS WAS MY PERSONAL CAR FOR 18+ YEARS. PRICE IS CASH OR BANK WIRE TRANSFER ONLY, NO TRADES, NO FINANCING. Now that you read through all of that, here are the details about the car. 2005 Pontiac GTO 6.0 LS2, APS Twin Turbo, 6spd, 68k 2 owner miles, I have owned this car since 2007 and only put roughly 8k miles on it! Past couple of years I have only taken it to the track a few times. Walk around video. http://youtu.be/o7NnjUc_VuI?si=TGMUtpJ6PeSGuwrj Last pass at track before Viking Adjustable Shocks. http://youtu.be/jtGL35nrURE?si=TdXR3eU1v37Zmx0E Any questions Call Rob-215......554.......8443 10am-4pm Eastern Time. NO TXTS! Best run about 2years ago was at only 7lbs of boost,10.85@129mph spinning before I upgraded to Viking adjustable rear shocks. I haven't taken it back to the track this year but should be capable in it's current form to do 10.60-10.70s with no other changes. I have a large collection of cars and it is time to start passing them on to people that will drive and enjoy them. I am a serious seller and want this car to go to a good home. The car has a clean and clear PA title ready to be transferred. If you are a PA resident you will be required to transfer the car into your name before it leaves my garage with your valid state ID or Drivers License. Out of state buyers will need to provide a valid DL or ID as well so title can be re-assigned to you and notarized. Transfer costs, Sales Tax and Tags will be the responsibility of the buyer and there are several Auto Tag places close by where this can be done during normal business hours. If you are having the car shipped I can assist your shipper with pick up and I may also be able to get you a great price on shipping via my dealer network of shippers. Current Turbos can go up to about 12lbs with different waste gate springs and tuning. Garage kept it's whole life, almost mint. has a few minor scratches and chips on the hood. Interior is mint. Windshield has 2 small chips. Upgrades: APS Twin Turbo System @7lbs. Deka 60lb injectors Billet Fuel Rails Walbro 340 pump MSD wires NGK plugs Upgraded and rebuilt T56 by The Gear Box. Monster Clutch Billet Flywheel Scatter Shield Custom direct Hurst Shifter 1 piece aluminum driveshaft G force axles and stubs Viking Adjustable shocks Need to be installed but included Pedders 90/10 front drag struts BMR Drag Springs and inflatable drag bags Harrop Diff Cover OEM Holden triple gauges Custom Autometer AFR/Fuel Pressure/Boost gauges in console Lingenfelter 2 step LLoyds GTO embroidered Floor mats including trunk 3" Stainless works exhaust to rear of car with no cats/mufflers, not too loud. Rides on Jegs Aluminum Centerline knock offs with 28.10.50x15 Drag slicks and front runners. *****I DO NOT HAVE FACTORY RIMS or any other parts!***** I may be forgetting some stuff.....but anything I could upgrade I did. No test pilots! Thanks for looking. Rob

title: Excellent condition Subaru Forester 5-speed manual trans.
description: This SUV needs nothing, it's completely turn-key. I just brought it to the mechanic* to check the fluids (brake, clutch, transmission, etc) and the brake pads. The mechanic said the brake pads are at 40% and the fluids are all fine, there was nothing for him to do. He recommended pads should be replaced this summer along with the oil change. Beyond that, the car needs absolutely nothing. It has new high and low-beam LEDs that increase visibility without blinding oncoming vehicles. Also recently equipped with top of the line fog lights by Diode Dynamics (SS3 pods along with vehicle-specific Diode Dynamics wiring harness) for enhanced visibility when driving in the fog and snow. Fog lights are amber in color and offer two brightness settings. They are wired to an in-cabin switch near the interior light dimmer. I've also replaced the turn signals with amber LEDs as well as the brake lights. Replaced: Axle shaft and CV joint, lower control arms, steering rack, sway bar, outer tie rod, front axle in May 2025. New cabin filter, steering wheel cover, clutch/gas/brake pedals, seat covers. Brand new tires, installed at Walmart, comes with lifetime tire balancing. 2,400lb capacity tow hitch. I’ve pulled a U-Haul trailer with zero issues. Bluetooth, CD player, AM/FM/Sat radio. Controls for calls, music controls, and cruise control on steering wheel. Regular full synthetic oil changes, proactively maintained, all parts replaced/repaired at regular intervals. Has full spare tire and jack, never been used. Has aux input and USB jack (in arm rest). Has additional 12v/120w cigarette lighter/auxiliary outlet in rear cargo area. No rust, 2nd owner, regularly washed including undercarriage. Terrific in all the snow we’ve had this winter. Will be great in the spring showers too. My mom is giving me her 2018 Forester, the last year they made it with manual transmission, so I need to pass this on to someone else who will appreciate and take good care of it. Terrific vehicle. This model is PZEV meaning that it's designed to meet California's strict air quality standards by producing exceptionally low exhaust emissions and zero fuel evaporative emissions. Blemishes: superficial scratches on rear bumper, headlights slightly hazy due to age but can be buffed out ($40), the new LED headlights make that just a cosmetic issue as nighttime visibility could not be better. Original owner replaced cargo area floor with wood, I covered in cowhide so it would look better and make a nicer surface for transporting (see pix). Never had an issue with it. Car has been used as an SUV is intended, it’s made many trips to Lowes and Home Depot and hauled some wood and cinder blocks. As a result there are some scratches on the back of the arm rest, some marks on the interior fabric (under the seat covers). Size is perfect for navigating the city or country, it can squeeze into small parking spots but you can also throw your road bike in the back with some camping gear (or an inflatable twin mattress) and hit the road. If you continue to stay on top of regular oil changes and basic maintenance this car will easily take you over 300k miles. *happy to provide mechanic contact info to confirm service records

description: 20-Year Ownership: 1999 BMW 540i Sport - Biarritz Blue Metallic Description: It’s time to part with my beloved 540i Sport. title: 2007 Toyota Matrix XR - manual transmission
description: VIN: 2T1KR32E87C668590 Happy to share carfax to serious buyers. Selling my 2007 Toyota Matrix XR, manual transmission. This car was gifted to me at 110k miles by my uncle who took immaculate care of it, and I'm only selling it 10k miles later because I'm starting a family soon and want a bigger car. During my ownership I did a lot of maintenance and upgrades to ensure this car would last to 200k miles and beyond: 1. 4 new all-season tires (Michelin cross climate 2) - $1,000 2. Replaced clutch and throw out bearing - $1,300 3. Replaced struts/shocks on both front and back suspension. 4. Replaced front brakes (rotors and pads) 5. Replaced spark plugs 6. Replaced serpentine belt 7. Flushed brake fluid, coolant, and transmission fluid. I've also replaced the old radio/head unit with a modern screen infotainment center and backup camera that supports for Apple CarPlay and Android Auto. Things to be aware of: 1. There is a small, cosmetic dent on the right side of the vehicle behind the passenger door. 2. There is a little bit of squeaking from the engine bay. I believe it's one of the engine pulleys that need to be replaced. 3. You can feel the vibration of the engine in the steering wheel. The fix to this is to replace the engine mounts which I have purchased and will include in the sale of the vehicle ($500), I just haven't had time to install them yet.

title: BMW 2013 - 128i M sport
description: BMW 128i - M Sport 2013 The Msport model is the upgraded version of the standard 128i 230hp M style front and rear bumpers and upgraded suspension plus, plus.. This car is perfectly suited for a driver wanting to experience track days, autocross or wanting to drive better in a super safe car. I didn’t build this car for power I built it with “safety first” in mind, all airbags are in place and fully functional. It is fully road legal with No oil leaks No rust. M style paddle shifters and steering wheel. Harmon Kardon CD player. Full leather sport / power seats. Sunroof. Carbon fiber rear lip spoiler. Upgraded the suspension to ST adjustable coilovers with camber plates. Falken 660RT summer tires. Stainless steel brake lines and race wheel studs. Auto power roll bar, the rear seat was removed to ensure correct fitment of the roll-bar. (Rear seat and side panels will accompany sale) Rear muffler delete (original will accompany sale) TPMS sensors removed in order to accommodate different tire pressures and reprogramed to show a flat tire only. This was originally a Florida car and then its second owner was in Georgia. I purchased the car in Atlanta three years ago. Engine and power train is completely stock. Previous owner replaced valve cover gasket and oil filter housing gasket. Recent work new thrust / control arms (upgraded version) and engine mounts. Clean car fax. Some paint fade and stone chips which be can expected with the age and mileage of the car. $12,500

title: 2016 Volkswagen GTI SE — Sport Package | Lighting Package
description: 2016 Volkswagen GTI SE — Sport Package | Lighting Package | Meticulous Maintenance History If you've been burned by an unmaintained GTI before, this is the one you've been waiting for. This 2016 GTI SE with Sport and Lighting packages has been maintained obsessively to the official VW 2.0 TFSI maintenance schedule (link: https://static.nhtsa.gov/odi/tsbs/2019/MC-10161298-0001.pdf) — oil changes every 5,000 miles, brake fluid replaced on the 3-year cycle, and DSG transmission fluid and filter serviced every 40,000 miles, no exceptions. The known weak points of the MK7 platform have all been addressed: High-pressure fuel pump (HPFP) replaced at 120,000 miles Water pump replaced at 90,000 miles Low-pressure fuel pump (LPFP) replaced at 115,000 miles under recall This isn't a car that was driven until something broke — it's a car that had things replaced before they could. The result is a GTI that drives exactly the way a well-sorted hot hatch should: tight, responsive, and confidence-inspiring. The Sport Package adds the limited-slip differential and larger brakes, making this more than a boulevard cruiser — it's the version serious drivers spec'd. The Lighting Package brings the LED DRLs and cornering lights that make night driving noticeably better. I didn't keep physical service records (my bad), but the maintenance history should be reflected on the Carfax — oil changes, pump replacements, and the LPFP recall work were all done at shops that report to Carfax.

title: 2012 Toyota Sienna XLE with a lot of extra stuff
description: Hi, here is 2012 Toyota Sienna XLE. This car never let me down. I bought it in 2022 from 1st owner when it was 98k miles, now is 173k miles. Clean title. Inspection until 03/27, Mechanically 4/5, everything works as it should, one of engine mounts needs  to be replaced but now it's almost unnoticeable, also brake rotors and pads will needs to be replaced soon. Through 4 years I did a lot to keep this car mechanically well maintained and all its documented: - transmission service - new AC compressor with all radiators and equipment - front struts, axles, lower arms, links, tie rods, all wheel bearings, rear struts I have a lot of extra stuff in boxes for this Toyota, I hadn't time to do it last time: - two new  17" OEM rims - new front headlights - rear left taillight (existing has a crack) - tie rods with links - fueling access door - window sunguards - 4 new air pressure sensors - new set of spray paint - new front seat covers - engine air filter and cabin filter This is over $1000 in new extra stuff. Roof box not included. You can see this car in Howard Beach or Greenpoint during the day. Good car for small contractor or handyman, small delivery service etc.

title: 2002 GMC Sierra 2500HD Truck
description: About 118,000 miles.  Engine is in excellent condition, as are wheels & tires, and 4x4.  All components of front-end brakes are brand new.  Transmission is not operating well, so that's where the work would be, unless you want it for all of the parts.  The automatic upshifting is delayed, and quite coarse.  Can get you home, but should be repaired for use.  Bed is 6-1/2 ft long.  Has plow mount, and bed cover is in great condition.  For anyone with an appetite to rebuild the transmission, this could be a workhorse truck.  Aesthetically, body rust in the usual places for a 20+ year old truck.
